question.text: Under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, the competent authority shall endeavour to pass an appropriate order within what time frame?

answer.text: Within 120 days from the date of submission or personal hearing, whichever is later
Within 180 days from the date of initiation of proceedings
Within 90 days from the date of personal hearing
Within 60 days from the date of submission

explanation.text: **The Logic:** After considering facts, material on record, and written submissions, the competent authority shall endeavour to pass an appropriate order within 120 days from the date of submission or personal hearing, whichever is later. **The Trap:** The starting point is the later of two dates (submission or hearing), not the earlier or the initiation date. **Cross-Exam Context:** Order-passing timelines are tested in NISM Series III-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 120-day window means that disciplinary proceedings can take several months from start to finish - factor this into your compliance risk planning.
